title: 10 best herbal tinctures for gum pain

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tinctures helps with gum pain because it possesses anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tannins present in sage tincture also help to numb the affected area, providing relief from pain and sensitivity. Additionally, sage's antibacterial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to gum pain. By using sage tinctures, individuals can effectively alleviate gum pain and promote healthy gums and a refreshing smile.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with gum pain.
Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial compounds in sage tincture, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain and inflammation.
Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture's antioxidant properties, including its high concentration of polyphenols, neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound that exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene that has potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat gum infections and reduce pain and inflammation.
Salvianolic acid A: A phenolic acid that has strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce gum inflammation, prevent tissue damage, and alleviate pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain salvene and other compounds that exhibit pain-relieving properties, making them effective in tinctures for gum pain.
Roots: Rich in salvinic acid and other active compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties useful for gum pain relief.
Leaves: Also contain camphor and thujone, which have local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory effects, further contributing to their use in gum pain tinctures.
remedy_recipe: Harvest salvia officinalis leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place at 30-40 degree celsius for 1 week.
Combine 50 grams of dried salvia officinalis leaves with 250 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 4 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and discard the solids for final product.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wood sage tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. The herbal extract also has antimicrobial properties that combat bacteria and fungi that can contribute to gum inflammation. Additionally, wood sage's analgesic properties help numb the affected area, providing quick relief from sharp pains and aching sensations. By soothing and calming the gums, wood sage tinctures provide natural and effective relief from gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds: Wood sage tincture contains bioactive compounds like thujone, borneol, and camphor that have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and combat infections in the gums.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: The tincture's active ingredients, such as camphor and thujone, have a direct impact on pain pathways, reducing pain perception and providing temporary relief from gum discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Wood sage tincture contains antioxidants that can help to neutralize free radicals in the mouth, protecting gum tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by inflammation, which can contribute to gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Diterpenoids: These compounds, specifically teucrin and teucrin A, have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate gum pain by reducing inflammation and preventing infections in the gums.

Flavonoids: Teucrium polium contains flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce gum pain by protecting the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Limonoids: Limonoids present in Teucrium polium, such as teucrium polium limonoids, possess anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can contribute to the relief of gum pain by reducing inflammation and pain in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary part used due to their high content of essential oils, flavonoids, and other bioactive compounds that help alleviate gum pain and inflammation.
Roots: Roots are used for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce gum pain and swelling.
Stems: Stems are used due to their presence of bioactive compounds that help in reducing inflammation and pain in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of the aerial parts of the plant in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material in a warm place with good air circulation for 1-2 weeks.
Combine 1 part of the dried plant material with 2 parts of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking every 2 days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cornflower tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in cornflower have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums, making it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and pain. Additionally, cornflower's antibacterial properties help combat plaque and prevent further infection, promoting healthy gums and reducing pain caused by abscesses or cavities. By addressing the underlying causes of gum pain, herbal cornflower tinctures provide effective relief from discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cornflower contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate gum pain caused by inflammation.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in cornflower tincture, such as quercetin and kaempferol, help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum pain and discomfort.

Antimicrobial properties: Cornflower's antimicrobial compounds, like rosmarinic acid, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, leading to pain and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Chlorogenic acid: This phenolic compound helps with gum pain by reducing inflammation and exhibiting antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may cause gum pain.
Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum pain by reducing inflammation and swelling in the affected area.

Kaempferol: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, potentially reducing gum pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are used to make tinctures for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe gum pain and infections.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make tinctures for their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent infections in the gums.
Seeds: The seeds are used to make tinctures for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and fight infections in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/2 cup of fresh or 1 cup of dried flowers early morning when they are dry.
Combine the harvested flowers with 2 cups of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass jar.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for 6 months before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's antimicrobial compounds also help combat bacteria that can cause infections, leading to chronic gum pain. Additionally, pot marigold's analgesic properties provide relief from acute toothaches and sensitivity. By soothing and calming the gums, this natural remedy offers a gentle yet effective solution for alleviating gum pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenes, which help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort.
Antibacterial properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in pot marigold have antibacterial properties, which help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain and disease.

Wound-healing activity: Pot marigold's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds also promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving tissue regeneration, and reducing scarring, all of which can help to alleviate gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds help with gum pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing in the affected area.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and ferulic acid): These compounds help with gum pain by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to alleviate pain and prevent infection.
Saponins: These compounds help with gum pain by having anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce swelling and relieve pain in the gums.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used because they contain the highest concentration of bioactive compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: They are used because they contain a similar profile of bioactive compounds as the flowers, but are often more accessible and easier to harvest.

Stems: They are used because they contain a smaller amount of bioactive compounds compared to the flowers and leaves, but are still valued for their anti-inflammatory properties and are often used in combination with other parts.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the flowers of calendula officinalis in full bloom on a sunny day when the petals are fully open.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Prepare the dried flowers by crushing them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the powdered flowers with 100 milliliters of 80 percent ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking it occasionally to facilitate extraction.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's active compounds, such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, work to soothe irritated gum tissues and alleviate sensitivity. Additionally, licorice root has been shown to have antimicrobial properties, helping to combat gum infections that can cause pain and inflammation. By using herbal licorice tinctures, individuals can experience natural relief from gum discomfort and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and ease gum pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory compounds.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains various flavonoids, such as isoliquiritigenin and licoricidin, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to soothe gum irritation and prevent further inflammation.

Triterpenoids: The triterpenoid fraction of licorice root has been shown to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Glycyrrhizin: This triterpene saponin has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe and protect the gums from infection and inflammation.
Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the gums, promoting healing and pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, are sometimes used in place of roots and contain similar medicinal properties.
Barks: The barks are occasionally used for their potential analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, although they may contain lower levels of glycyrrhizin compared to roots and rhyzomes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the summer months when they are at peak potency.
Dry the roots in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days at 50 degrees fahrenheit.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine them with 70 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 6 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking every day.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ceylon cinnamon tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, such as cinnamic acid and cinnamate, have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. When taken internally as a tincture, these compounds can help soothe gum tissue and reduce swelling, providing relief from toothaches, gum recession, and other gum-related discomforts. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon's antimicrobial properties may also help combat gum infections and promote healthy oral flora, further contributing to its effectiveness in addressing gum pain.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ceylon cinnamon's antioxidants neutralize free radicals, which helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: Cinnamon contains cinnamaldehyde, a potent anti-inflammatory compound that reduces swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from gum pain and inflammation.

Natural analgesic properties: Ceylon cinnamon's tincture has been shown to exhibit natural analgesic properties, which can help numb or reduce the sensation of pain in the gums, providing temporary relief.
remedy_constituents: Eugenol: Eugenol acts as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent, helping to numb gum pain and reduce inflammation in the affected area.

Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde possesses analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate gum pain and discomfort by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation.

Cinnamyl acetate: Cinnamyl acetate has antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the spread of infection and promote healing in gum tissue, thereby reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum infections or inflammation.
remedy_parts: Barks: Used due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe gum inflammation and prevent infection.
Leaves: Utilized for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate gum pain and reduce swelling.
Buds: Employed for its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial and fungal infections that can cause gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 pounds of fresh cinnamomum verum bark in the morning or late afternoon.
Chop 2 cups of the harvested bark into small pieces to increase surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped bark with 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's antimicrobial agents also combat bacteria that can cause infection and further exacerbate gum pain. Additionally, Kansas coneflower contains compounds that have natural analgesic and antiseptic properties, providing relief from sensitivity and soreness. Antimicrobial properties: Kansas Coneflower contains antibacterial and antifungal compounds that can help combat infections in the mouth, such as gum disease-causing bacteria, reducing pain and discomfort associated with gum infections.

Immune system support: The tincture's immune-boosting properties may help stimulate the body's natural defenses to fight off oral pathogens, including those responsible for gum disease and related pain.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators, which contribute to gum pain.
Alkaloids (including echinatin and echinacoside): These compounds may have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help alleviate gum pain and discomfort.
Flavonoids (including quercetin): These compounds may have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which could help reduce gum inflammation and alleviate pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are commonly used to make tinctures for gum pain due to their high concentration of alkaloids, specifically echinacosides, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Echinacea angustifolia leaves are sometimes used to make tinctures for gum pain due to their potential to stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation.
Roots: Echinacea angustifolia roots are also used to make tinctures for gum pain due to their ability to reduce swelling and fight infection, which can contribute to gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of echinacea angustifolia in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Clean and chop the roots into small pieces weighing around 250 grams for a standard batch.
Mix the chopped roots with 500 milliliters of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The herb's flavonoids and hyperforin also have analgesic and antispasmodic effects, helping to alleviate toothaches, sensitivity, and pain caused by gum recession or inflammation. remedy_constituents: Naphthodianthrones: These compounds have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh st. john's wort flowers and leaves in the summer months when they are in full bloom.
Chop the fresh st. john's wort flowers and leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine one part of the chopped st. john's wort with one part of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking it every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's active compounds, including salicin and flavonoids, work to alleviate inflammation and soothe irritated gum tissue. Additionally, guelder rose tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum pain. Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness in the gums, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.

Fruits: The fruits are used due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to promote healing and reduce pain in the gums.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part of dried viburnum opulus flowers and 2 parts of root in a clean container.
Combine the plant material in a clean glass jar with 80% high proof neutral grain spirit.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 weeks to allow the tincture to mature.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 4 weeks before using it.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. The tincture's active compounds, including thujone and sesquiterpene lactones, work to alleviate inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause pain and swelling. Additionally, wormwood's antimicrobial properties help combat gum infections, further contributing to its ability to ease gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Wormwood's antimicrobial properties can help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacteria and other pathogens that may be contributing to gum pain, allowing for a faster recovery.

remedy_parts: Leaves: Artemisia absinthium leaves are used in tinctures for gum pain due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: The stems of Artemisia absinthium are used in tinctures for gum pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of Artemisia absinthium are used in tinctures for gum pain due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of the aerial parts of artemisia absinthium on a dry sunny day.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and dry it further in low-temperature oven at 30c for 2 hours.
Combine 100 grams of the dried plant material with 750 ml of 95% ethanol in a glass container.
Steep the plant material in the ethanol for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the final tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural analgesic properties. The methyl salicylate found in wintergreen has a similar composition to aspirin, which is known for its ability to reduce inflammation and ease pain. When applied topically as a tincture, the active compounds in wintergreen are absorbed quickly into the affected area, providing quick relief from gum discomfort. Additionally, wintergreen's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and alleviate pressure on sensitive gum tissue, making it an effective natural remedy for managing gum pain.
remedy_properties: Methyl salicylate: This key compound in wintergreen has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective in reducing gum pain and swelling by blocking the production of prostaglandins, which are chemicals that cause pain and inflammation.

Glycosides: Wintergreen's glycosides have been shown to exhibit antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent infections and reduce the risk of tooth decay or gum disease, both common causes of gum pain.

Astringent properties: The astringent properties of wintergreen tincture can help to reduce bleeding and inflammation in the gums, making it useful for treating gingivitis and periodontal disease, which are major contributors to gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Wintergreen roots contain a high concentration of methyl salicylate, a natural pain reliever and anti-inflammatory compound that effectively treats gum pain.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the wintergreen plant are rich in menthol and other volatile oils that provide analgesic and antiseptic properties to soothe gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems at dawn when they are most potent.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and weigh out 100 grams for the batch.
Combine the chopped plant material with 500 milliliters of 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 weeks to allow for maximum extraction of active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and transfer it to amber glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed tinctures helps with gum pain because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The herb contains compounds like parthenolide and sesquiterpenes that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort in the gums and surrounding tissues. When used as a tincture, these active ingredients are absorbed quickly into the bloodstream, providing rapid relief from gum pain and swelling. Antioxidant properties: The tannins and flavonoids present in Joe Pye Weed help to protect against oxidative stress and damage to gum tissue, reducing the risk of gum recession and pain.
Pain-relieving compounds: Joe Pye Weed contains salicylic acid, a natural analgesic that helps to reduce pain and discomfort in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots of Joe Pye weed have been traditionally used to treat oral health issues and provide relief from gum pain.
Barks: The bark of Joe Pye weed contains salicin, a natural compound similar to aspirin, which helps reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality eupatorium perfoliatum leaves and stems, weighing approximately one pound for this recipe.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to ensure maximum surface area exposure during the extraction process.
Combine the chopped plant material with 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, using a 1:5 ratio by weight of herb to solvent.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate efficient solvent penetration and extraction.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the liquid extract.

Seeds: The seeds have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat oral health issues, including gum pain, due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried solidago virgaurea leaves and stems in the morning for maximum potency.
Combine one part of the plant material with two parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar tightly and place it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks to steep.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle after the steeping period.
Label and store the final tincture in a cool dark place, using one to two droppers full (1/2 teaspoon) three times a day for gum pain relief.

remedy_recipe: Gather 20-30 grams of dried taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar.
Pour 80-100 proof vodka over the herb mixture ensuring it is completely covered.
Label the jar and date it then store it in a cool dark place for four weeks allowing the tincture to mature.
After the maturation period strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the resulting herbal extract into dark glass dropper bottles and label them with ingredients dosages and instructions.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial activity of yarrow tincture can help combat bacteria that may be contributing to gum pain, such as periodontal disease or tooth abscesses, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Analgesic and anodyne properties: Yarrow tincture's analgesic and anodyne compounds, including acetophenone and borneol, have been shown to help reduce pain and discomfort in the gums, making it an effective natural remedy for gum-related pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can help soothe gum pain.
Flowers: The flowers of yarrow are rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that can help combat gum infections and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop the plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction and transfer it to a clean glass jar.
Add 70% ethanol vodka or neutral grain spirits as the solvent in a ratio of 1 part plant material to 5 parts solvent.
Store the mixture in a dark place at room temperature for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and maceration.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and label it with the date and contents for future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tinctures helps with gum pain because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The mucilage extracted from the marshmallow plant coats and protects the gums, reducing irritation and discomfort. Additionally, the herbal extract's ability to reduce swelling and inflammation can alleviate painful symptoms associated with gum disease, gingivitis, and periodontitis. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to provide relief from gum pain and promote healthy oral mucosa.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Marshmallow tincture helps reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, alleviating pain and discomfort caused by conditions such as gingivitis or periodontal disease.

Soothing and protective mucilage properties: The sticky mucilage in marshmallow tincture forms a barrier that coats and soothes irritated gum tissue, providing relief from pain, irritation, and inflammation while also protecting the gums from further damage.

Antioxidant properties: Marshmallow tincture's antioxidant properties help combat free radicals that can contribute to gum disease and inflammation, reducing oxidative stress and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Marshmallow roots have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, making them effective in reducing gum inflammation and pain.
Barks: The barks of the marshmallow plant contain mucilages that help to reduce swelling and provide relief from gum irritation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis roots and leaves, using scissors or pruning shears to collect fresh material.
Rinse the plant material with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris, then pat dry with paper towels.
Chop the clean plant material into small pieces, ensuring uniformity for efficient extraction, and transfer to a clean glass jar.
Combine 1 part althaea officinalis material with 2 parts vodka in the glass jar, seal tightly, and store in a cool dark place for 14 days.
Strain the liquid mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding solids, and label as finished tincture ready for use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Beach rose tinctures helps with gum pain because its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and soothe irritated gum tissue. The tincture's antimicrobial agents also work to combat bacteria that can cause gum infections, leading to pain and discomfort. Additionally, the beach rose's natural analgesic properties provide quick relief from gum pain, making it an effective natural remedy for managing oral health issues. By using herbal beach rose tinctures, individuals can promote healthy gums and alleviate persistent pain.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Beach rose tincture has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which helps combat bacteria and fungi that can cause gum infections and inflammation, ultimately reducing gum pain.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tannins present in beach rose tincture possess anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum pain and inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: Beach rose tincture is rich in antioxidants that help neutralize free radicals and protect the gums from oxidative damage, thereby reducing gum pain and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Beach Rose roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in the gums.
Barks: The barks of the Beach Rose plant have analgesic properties, making them effective in reducing pain caused by gum inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rosa rugosa flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency and flavor.
Combine one part dried rosa rugosa flowers with three parts high-proof vodka or neutral grain spirit in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the plant compounds to infuse into the solvent.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids and reserve the tincture for use.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids and store in the refrigerator for up to one year, shaking well before using as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tinctures helps with gum pain because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The tannins present in lemon balm have been shown to reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums, providing relief from dental procedures and other oral health issues. Additionally, lemon balm's calming effects can help to soothe the nerves and muscles surrounding the gums, reducing sensitivity and discomfort. As a result, many people find that using herbal lemon balm tinctures helps to alleviate gum pain and promote overall oral wellness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lemon balm contains rosmarinic acid, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with gum pain.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of lemon balm may help combat bacterial infections in the mouth, such as periodontitis, which can contribute to gum pain.

Anxiolytic and sedative: Lemon balm's anxiolytic and sedative properties can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common factors that exacerbate gum pain, allowing for a more relaxing and healing environment.

remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves at dawn on a sunny day for maximum potency.
Chop 250 grams of fresh or dried melissa officinalis leaves into fine pieces.
Combine chopped melissa officinalis with 750 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for eight weeks to allow for maceration and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter after eight weeks into another clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain. Additionally, chamomile's natural relaxant effects can help to calm gum tissue spasms, reducing sensitivity and soreness. This natural remedy can be easily administered by placing a few drops of the tincture under the tongue or adding it to water for a soothing rinse.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant agents, including flavonoids and terpenoids, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum damage and pain, promoting healthy tissue and reducing inflammation.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Chamomile's soothing and calming effects can help alleviate stress and anxiety, which are common triggers for gum pain, allowing the individual to relax and reduce their discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it effective in reducing gum inflammation and preventing infections.
Flowers: Containing azulene, an essential oil with analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to soothe gum pain and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the fresh flowers by spreading them out on paper bags or clean cloths.
Combine one part of the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with three parts of high-proof vodka.
Steep the mixture for four to six weeks, shaking the container daily, before filtering and straining.
Store the tincture in dark glass bottles and use within two years or after two weeks of opening.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for gum pain are concentrated liquid extracts from plants that help alleviate discomfort and inflammation in the gums. They work by reducing pain, inflammation, and bacterial growth. The best herbal tinctures for gum pain include Echinacea purpurea, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Melaleuca alternifolia, Salvia officinalis, Achillea millefolium, Eucalyptus globulus, Mentha x piperita, Hydrastis canadensis, and Gaultheria procumbens. If left untreated, gum pain can lead to difficulty eating, speaking, and even social isolation due to embarrassment and discomfort, significantly impacting one's quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gum pain that are treated with herbal tinctures are various oral and dental issues, such as toothaches, abscesses, and mouth ulcers. Herbal tinctures like Calendula, Tea Tree, and Echinacea have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help soothe and heal these conditions. Additionally, tinctures of Ginger, Peppermint, and Sage may also provide relief from gum pain by reducing inflammation and easing discomfort.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with gum pain is a blend of Calendula, Echinacea, and Peppermint. Calendula tincture has anti-inflammatory properties to reduce swelling, while Echinacea helps boost the immune system to fight off infections. Peppermint tincture provides a cooling sensation to numb the pain. Additionally, incorporating Clove oil or Myrrh tincture can also aid in reducing inflammation and promoting healing of the gums. These herbal tinctures can be combined and applied topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Iranian journal of basic medical sciences, herbal tinctures have been found to be beneficial for gum pain due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The study reviewed 18 clinical trials on herbal products for gingivitis, with specific attention to the efficacy of pomegranate, aloe, green tea, and miswak. The results showed that these herbal products significantly reduced gingival inflammation and bleeding, and improved different indices of oral hygiene.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tannins and phenolic acids in Echinacea purpurea tincture also have antimicrobial effects, preventing bacterial growth that can cause gum infections. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tincture's antioxidant properties help combat oxidative stress, promoting healing and reducing inflammation in the gums. As a result, Echinacea purpurea tincture can provide relief from gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline Alkaloids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and pain in gum tissue by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Caffeic Acid Derivatives: As potent antioxidants, these compounds help protect gum tissue from oxidative stress and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating gum pain.
Alkylamides: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate gum pain.
Flowers: They have antiseptic properties that can help reduce inflammation and prevent infection in the gums.
Leaves: They contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacteria causing gum pain and infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at peak potency in late summer.
Chop the roots and flowers into small pieces and combine them in a clean glass jar.
Steep the plant material in 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:3 for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear tincture.
Transfer the tincture to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort in the gums. The active compound, gingerol, has natural analgesic and antiseptic properties that soothe irritated gum tissue. By reducing inflammation and promoting healing, Zingiber officinale tinctures can provide relief from gum pain and discomfort. This makes it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation, toothaches, and other oral health issues. Regular use can lead to improved oral health and reduced pain.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Shogaols: Shogaols have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in alleviating gum pain and discomfort.

Volatile oils (mainly zingiberene): These oils possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help combat gum infections and reduce inflammation, ultimately providing relief from gum pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Utilized to create tinctures for gum pain relief due to their rich content of bioactive compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation.
Buds: Employed in tincture production for gum pain as they contain volatile oils, including gingerol and shogaol, that possess analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 roots of zingiber officinale, clean and chop them into small pieces, and set them aside for 2 hours.
Combine the chopped roots with 80-proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio, and store the mixture in a glass jar.
Place the glass jar in a cool dark place and allow the mixture to steep for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass container, and discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid tincture into dark glass bottles with dropper lids, and store them in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The tincture contains compounds like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have been shown to reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that may be contributing to the pain. By reducing inflammation and preventing infection, Glycyrrhiza glabra tinctures can provide relief from gum pain and promote a healthy oral environment. This can lead to improved oral health and comfort.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: Acts as an anti-inflammatory agent to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Flavonoids: Possess antioxidant properties to protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, thus alleviating pain.
Saponins: Exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties to combat bacterial infections and inflammation in the gums, leading to pain relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound known to have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate gum pain.
Leaves: The leaves are also used, containing smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin, which contribute to the tincture's overall anti-inflammatory effects.
Barks: The barks of the plant contain smaller amounts of glycyrrhizin compared to the roots, but are still used in some tinctures to provide additional anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Gather 500g of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and store them in a cool dry place for later use.
Combine the dried roots with 70% ethanol in a 1:3 ratio to create the tincture base.
Allow the mixture to steep in a glass container for 14 days in a cool dark place.
Shake the container daily for 2 minutes to facilitate extraction and mixing of the tincture.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a clear herbal tincture.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia tinctures helps with gum pain because of its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture, derived from the leaves of the Melaleuca tree, contains compounds like cineole and terpinen-4-ol that have been shown to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria that can cause gum infections. This makes it an effective natural remedy for gum pain and inflammation. Additionally, its antibacterial properties help prevent the spread of infection, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing discomfort associated with gum pain.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps reduce inflammation and ease gum pain by acting as an anti-inflammatory and anesthetic agent.
Terpinen-4-ol: Exhibits antimicrobial properties, which help prevent bacterial growth and infection that can cause gum pain.
Cymene: Shows analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds, particularly eucalyptol, which has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: The buds are used to create tinctures due to their high oil content, which includes compounds like eucalyptol and caryophyllene, that help reduce gum inflammation and pain.

Stems: The stems are another part used for tincture production, as they contain essential oils that exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in alleviating gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh leaves from mature plants of melaleuca alternifolia in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Wash the leaves gently with cool water to remove any dirt or debris from the surface.
Combine 250 grams of the cleaned leaves with 750 milliliters of 95% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tannins present in the herb act as a natural astringent, reducing inflammation and discomfort in the gums. Additionally, Salvia officinalis contains antioxidants that help combat oxidative stress, promoting overall oral health. By soothing and protecting the gums, Salvia officinalis tinctures can provide relief from gum pain and inflammation, making it a popular herbal remedy for oral health issues. remedy_constituents: Ursolic acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gum tissue.
Rosmarinic acid: As an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, rosmarinic acid helps alleviate gum pain by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Salvianolic acid: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help soothe gum pain and promote the recovery of gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Salvia officinalis contain bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate gum pain and inflammation.
Stems: The stems of Salvia officinalis contain compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for relieving gum pain and discomfort.

Buds: The buds of Salvia officinalis are rich in antioxidants and bioactive compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate gum pain, making them a popular ingredient in tinctures for gum health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers in the morning when they are at their highest potency levels.
Dry the harvested salvia leaves and flowers in a cool dark place for 7 to 10 days.
Combine 1 part of dried salvia officinalis with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily to release the active ingredients.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and store the tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Achillea millefolium tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The tincture's active compounds, such as sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, work to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that can cause gum pain. Additionally, its astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain. As a result, Achillea millefolium tinctures can provide natural relief from gum pain and promote overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Chlorogenic acid: Reduces inflammation and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Apigenin: Exerts anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which help to alleviate pain and swelling in the gums.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, providing relief from gum pain and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are commonly used due to their high concentration of compounds that have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Flowers: The flowers are often used because they contain flavonoids and terpenes that help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain.
Stems: The stems are utilized for their content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh achillea millefolium flowers and leaves in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material in a clean area or using a dehydrator at 95°f for 1-2 hours.
Chop 1 part of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves into small pieces using scissors or a sharp knife.
Steep 1 part of chopped plant material in 2-3 parts of 80% ethanol in a glass jar for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tincture contains compounds like eucalyptol, which has natural analgesic and antibacterial properties. It helps to combat the root cause of gum pain, such as inflammation and infection. The tincture also has a decongestant effect, reducing swelling and promoting healing. Regular use of Eucalyptus globulus tinctures may provide long-term relief from gum pain and promote healthy oral hygiene.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps with gum pain by reducing inflammation and relieving pain through its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Eugeniol: Helps with gum pain by exhibiting antimicrobial properties, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healing in the affected area.
Linalool: Helps with gum pain by displaying anti-inflammatory and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate discomfort and anxiety associated with gum pain.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part for making eucalyptus tinctures due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound.
Barks: Barks are also used to make eucalyptus tinctures, as they contain compounds like eucalyptol and flavonoids that help reduce pain and inflammation.
Stems: Some herbalists use stems of Eucalyptus globulus to make tinctures, as they contain a similar profile of bioactive compounds to the leaves and barks, although to a lesser extent.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and dry them in a warm dark place for 2 weeks.
Combine the dried leaves with 500ml of 70% ethanol in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks shaking the jar daily to ensure all parts are saturated with the liquid.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar discarding the solids.
Transfer the tincture to smaller dark glass bottles and store it in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita tinctures helps with gum pain because it contains menthol, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory compound. When applied topically, menthol helps to numb the affected area, reducing pain and discomfort. The antibacterial properties of peppermint also help to combat gum infections, promoting a healthy environment for gum tissue to heal. Additionally, menthol's vasoconstrictive properties help to reduce swelling, making it an effective remedy for gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Menthone: Menthone acts as a local anesthetic, numbing the affected area and reducing pain perception in the gums.
Menthol: Menthol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.
Limonene: Limonene has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections that may be causing the gum pain, thus promoting healing and reducing discomfort.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are most commonly used due to their high concentration of menthol and menthone, which provide analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to relieve gum pain.

Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are also used to make tinctures for gum pain, as they contain similar properties to the leaves, including menthol and menthone, which help to reduce pain and inflammation.

Flowers: The flowers of Mentha x piperita are sometimes used to make tinctures for gum pain, as they contain a lower concentration of menthol and menthone than the leaves or stems, but still provide some analgesic and anti-inflammatory benefits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the fresh leaves and flowers of mentha x piperita when they are at their peak potency and fragrance.
Clean the harvested mentha x piperita thoroughly with water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 part of the cleaned mentha x piperita with 2 parts of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrastis canadensis tinctures helps with gum pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb, also known as goldenseal, has been traditionally used to treat mouth and gum issues. The tannins and alkaloids present in goldenseal work to reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial growth, which can cause pain and discomfort in the gums. By reducing swelling and preventing infection, Hydrastis canadensis tinctures promote healthy gums and alleviate pain associated with gum problems. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to oral care routines.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums.
Vasaka alkaloids: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections in the gums and reduce associated pain.
Hydrastine: This alkaloid has analgesic properties, which can help numb the pain in the gums and provide relief.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the primary part used due to their high concentration of berberine, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help alleviate gum pain.
Leaves: The leaves are another commonly used part, as they contain similar compounds to the roots, which can help reduce inflammation and fight infections in the gums.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, are also utilized, as they contain berberine and other alkaloids that can help soothe gum pain and promote oral health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh root of hydrastis canadensis from a trusted source and wash it with filtered water.
Chop the fresh root into small pieces and combine it with 80 to 85 percent ethanol in a glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow the tincture to extract.
Shake the jar every day to ensure the tincture extracts evenly from the root.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Gaultheria procumbens tinctures helps with gum pain because it contains high levels of salicylic acid, a natural anti-inflammatory compound similar to aspirin. This acid helps to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums, making it an effective remedy for gum inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, Gaultheria procumbens has been traditionally used to address mouth sores, toothaches, and gum sensitivity, providing relief and promoting overall oral health. Its anti-inflammatory properties also aid in reducing bleeding and promoting healing.
remedy_constituents: Gentiopicroside: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Gentiopicrin: This constituent has analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from pain and inflammation in the gums.
Gaultherin: This compound has antioxidant properties, which help to protect the gums from oxidative stress and inflammation, reducing gum pain and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make tinctures for gum pain, as they contain salicin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate pain.
Roots: Used to make tinctures for gum pain, as they are rich in salicin and other compounds that have analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
Stems: Used to make tinctures for gum pain, as they may contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-40 ounces of gaultheria procumbens leaves and stems in early morning when they are at their highest moisture content.
Dry the harvested material in a temperature-controlled environment at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours to reduce moisture levels.
Use a mason jar with a 1:2 ratio of gaultheria procumbens to 80 proof vodka to create a tincture.
Steep the dried material in the vodka for 4-6 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard the solids after the steeping period is complete.
